title: Invite Yourself

meta.description: g0v Summit is centered on “Information Transparency, Open Results, and Open Collaboration,” we use the power of the grassroots to care for public affairs. In 2026, #🪑InviteYourself to Taipei to weave the future of civic space.

agenda.title: Agenda
agenda.info: The agenda is still being rolled out and is subject to change. Please check back for the most recent updates.
agenda.opass_info: You may find everything you need to know about the summit on the open-source OPass app (available on iOS & Android), where you can plan your schedule ahead of time and receive notifications as they’re about to start.

info.title: Traffic Info
info.venueName: Humanities & Social Sciences Building, Academia Sinica
info.address: 128 Academia Rd. Sec. 2, Nangang District, Taipei, Taiwan

publicTransportStep1.title: Reaching Nangang
publicTransportStep1.item1: Ride Taiwan Railway or Taiwan High Speed Rail to Nangang station.
publicTransportStep1.item2: Ride Taipei Metro to Nangang station (Bannan Line) or Nangang Exhibition Center station (Bannan Line & Wenhu Line).

publicTransportStep2.title: Reaching Academia Sinica
publicTransportStep2.item1: Summit Shuttle Bus operates daily from 7:50 to 9:30 between TRA Nangang Station ↔ North Entrance, HSSB, Academia Sinica with a 20-minute interval. Please keep an eye out for conference staff holding shuttle bus sign outside of the station and arrive early for an effortless ride to the venue.
publicTransportStep2.item2: From MRT Nangang station Exit 2, take route 212 Express, 270, or Blue 25 to Academia Sinica.
publicTransportStep2.item3: From MRT Nangang Exhibition Center station Exit 5, take route 205, 306, 620, 645, 679, S1, S5 or S12 to Academia Sinica.
publicTransportStep2.item4: Walk or ride YouBike to Humanities and Social Science Building.

drivingStep1.title: Driving Route
drivingStep1.item1: From National Highway No. 3, exit at Nanshen Rd., turn left onto Jiuzhuang St. Sec. 1, and then reach Academia Sinica on Academia Rd. Sec. 2.

drivingStep2.title: Vehicle Access
drivingStep2.item1: Drivers shall exchange a temporary parking permit at the entrance and park responsibly according to campus rules.

drivingStep3.title: Motorcycle Parking
drivingStep3.item1: As motorbikes are prohibited from entering the campus, please utilize free parking spaces next to Academia Sinica’s main entrance and side entrances.

cookieNotice: This website uses cookies to improve your experience. By continuing to use it, you consent to their use for advertising, social media, and analytics.

intro.title: "The Rope-Weaving Geometry of Invite Yourself!"
intro.p1: They say, "Once bitten by a snake, you’ll fear a rope for ten years." But at g0v Summit 2026, we say: ten years of <ruby>beating<rp>(</rp><rt>phah</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> the rope.
intro.p2: For g0v Summit 2026, this "rope" symbolizes the concrete expression of g0v’s decade of hacktivism: "Don’t ask why nobody is doing this — admit that you are nobody." Because "nobody" can do anything.
intro.p3: No waiting, no depending on others — we Invite Ourselves, and build things with our own hands:
intro.p4: Here, g0v Summit 2026 invites you into a three-dimensional civic coordinate system woven from rope.
intro.p5:
You and every participant, each grounded in your own skills, values, and positions, are a "point" in this space.
When we toss out our rope and engage with others, points become "lines";
when countless lines interweave, we hold up a "plane" — a dynamic network of action, a hammock that catches us all.
intro.p6: Isolated nodes form communities; single-thread conversations evolve into collaborative ecosystems.
intro.p7: From the original core of "information transparency, open results, open collaboration," g0v Summit 2026 evolves into a community-woven "civic coordinate system" with three axes: (Openness & Transparency, Autonomy & Privacy), (Critical Scrutiny & Resilient Connection), (Government Institutions & Grassroots Civil Society).
intro.p8: Here, no one is an outsider. Everyone can find their own coordinates — exploring inward, connecting outward.

venue.title: Venue
venue.name: Humanities and Social Sciences Building, Academia Sinica
venue.address: 128 Academia Rd. Sec. 2, Nangang District, Taipei, Taiwan

g0vSummit.title: What is g0v Summit?
g0vSummit.p1: Since 2012, g0v.tw, the Taiwan Zero-Hour Government community, under the rallying cry of "hacking to change society," has centered on "information transparency, open results, and open collaboration" — using the power of the grassroots to care for public affairs, launching over a decade of civic hacker practice.
g0vSummit.p2: Following the Sunflower Movement, g0v Summit (What?) was first held in 2014, bringing together open communities, government, NGOs, academia, and internet citizens for cross-sector exchange.
g0vSummit.p3: Over these decades, Taiwanese society has experienced countless democratic crises, social upheaval, and polarization — setbacks and crises like being "bitten by a snake."
g0vSummit.p4: Facing Taiwan’s societal wounds and threats from near and far, "ten years" is not just a measure of time — it etches the continuous action and practice of civic communities, the ongoing process of transforming fear into resilience.
g0vSummit.p5: They say, "Once bitten by a snake, you’ll fear a rope for ten years." But at g0v Summit 2026, we say "ten years of beating(phah) the rope". We believe in the power of grassroots communities and the bonds between us to continually transform the "fear" into the "action" of beating.
g0vSummit.p6: <ruby>Tying knots<rp>(</rp><rt>phah-kat</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby> is not just about binding tight — it is about learning to loosen: loosening passive postures, loosening dependence and fear, and in times of crisis and uncertainty, relearning the muscles of "self-rescue" and "mutual aid."
g0vSummit.p7: We refuse to be trapped in the passivity and timidity of "fearing the rope." Instead, g0v Summit 2026 invites everyone here to roll up their sleeves, reach out, grab that rope, and knead and beat it anew. We branch, weave, connect, and continuously transform and create — the "rope" is no longer a terrifying phantom, but a resilient and organic community.

joinG0v.title: Join g0v
joinG0v.p1: g0v is a grassroots civic community dedicated to deepening citizens’ contributions to society and the connections between them. Through the g0v community, you can find like-minded partners, put your ideas into practice in a grassroots way, and release your results under open licenses so that more people can build on your work.
joinG0v.p2: Replacing the "o" in "gov" with a zero to form g0v, we rethink the role of government from zero — representing the digital-native generation’s perspective from the world of 0s and 1s.

partners.title: Partners
partners.infolaw: Information Law Center, IIAS, Academia Sinica
partners.doit: Department of Information Technology, Taipei City Government

jothon.name: g0v Jothon
jothon.description: g0v Jothon is the task force responsible for organizing g0v’s Bi-monthly Hackathons and Infrathons, as well as managing the community’s fundraising affairs. Currently, the team consists of the Jothon Volunteer Group, project-based collaborators, and eight members of the Steering Committee.

friedrich.name: Friedrich-Naumann-Stiftung For Freedom
friedrich.description:
The Friedrich-Naumann-Stiftung for Freedom (FNF) is a German political foundation. In Germany and in 60 countries around the world, FNF and its partners promote freedom, liberalism, democracy, human rights, pluralism, tolerance, curbing climate change, an open and digital society, social market economy, and the rule of law. We are a non-profit organization that focuses on civic education.

We work with government institutions, NGOs, academia, political parties, researchers, students, journalists, activists, start-ups and artists who share our values and want to join us in promoting freedom. We work collaboratively and seek long-term partnerships, highlighting the importance of individual freedom and strong democratic institutions. FNF promotes economic freedom and free trade. We want businesses to respect human rights. We highly value privacy and data protection. FNF promotes curbing climate change by utilizing technological advances, for example in the context of smart cities.

FNF has worked in Asia since 1969. Today, we have five offices in Asia: Bangkok, Jarkata, Manila, Seoul, and Taipei. We also work in Malaysia.

In Taiwan, Taiwan Office/ Global Innovation Hub of the Friedrich Naumann Foundation for Freedom was established in 2021. The Hub focuses on “digital transformation” and “innovation for democracy.” With its international global network of experts, partners and collaborators, the hub aims to support the Friedrich Naumann Foundation on its way to becoming a creative innovation platform for liberalism.

luminate.name: Luminate
luminate.description: Luminate is a global foundation working to ensure that everyone – especially those who are underrepresented - has the information, rights, and power to influence the decisions that shape society. The foundation is focused on enabling people to fully participate in civic and political life, to safely challenge power, and to access accurate, trustworthy information. Luminate works globally with a regional focus on Africa, Asia, and Latin America. It was established by philanthropists Pierre and Pam Omidyar and has worked for over a decade on issues related to governance and citizen engagement.

support.title: Support g0v Summit
support.p1: g0v Summit is a volunteer-organized, non-profit event. “Invite Yourself” is our theme for 2026 — we invite everyone to pull up a stool and become part of the action. The sponsors listed above are the key supporters who make g0v Summit 2026 possible.
support.p2: Every g0v Summit depends on the support and participation of the community. Beyond attending sessions and events, you’re welcome to support the open community through sponsorship.
support.p3: We offer a variety of partnership packages including brand exposure, booth setup, ticket sponsorship, and more custom options. We look forward to working with you to advance civic technology.
support.p4: For sponsorship and partnership inquiries, please contact: sponsorship@summit.g0v.tw

qa.q1: 我的資料會被傳給數位發展部嗎？
qa.a1: 不會，發行與驗證伺服器的原始碼由數位發展部提供，並由 g0v Summit 志工於大會伺服器上架設並開發相關應用，發行證件所用的資料來自應援售票系統，證件資料僅會儲存於 App 內，報到資訊僅會留存於 g0v Summit 伺服器上並於大會後刪除。
qa.q2: 大會會索取我的哪些資料？
qa.a2: 證件上僅會有會眾已經於應援售票系統提供的資料，但不會全部放上。會放在證件上的與驗證過程索取的內容我們會遵從資料最小化的原則，只放上／索取必要資訊。例如透過數位憑證皮夾的選擇性揭露功能，報到時我們僅需索取能避免重複報到的欄位（如票號），而不需要證件上所有資料。在驗證資料送出前你可以在 App 上再度檢視我們索取的內容。若有其他應用場景，如領取便當，大會也僅需要知道你的餐飲資訊（葷／素），不需要索取額外的個資。
qa.q3: 為什麼你們不自行編譯 App 的原始碼讓會眾安裝或整合進 OPass？
qa.q4: 我一定要用數位憑證皮夾報到嗎？
qa.a4: 不用，我們理解不是每個人都有符合條件的手機能安裝，也可能有更嚴格的隱私考量，可以只以票號與報名資訊於現場進行報到。我們甚至提供現金購票與報到的選項。用現金購票者的資料完全不會經過應援或大會的線上系統，詳情請見官網與行前信！
